markdown:
Use **SWE-bench**. It is built specifically for evaluating systems that resolve real GitHub issues.

- SWE-bench contains 2,294 software engineering problems drawn from real GitHub issues and corresponding pull requests across 12 popular Python repositories. [1]
- Each task gives a model the issue text and a codebase snapshot; the model must produce a patch that is evaluated against the repository's real unit tests. [2]
- Tasks frequently require coordinating changes across multiple functions, classes, and files, and interacting with execution environments — i.e., exactly what an issue-fixing agent does. [1]
- It also includes SWE-bench-train (≈19,000 non-testing task instances from 37 repositories) if you want a training set for fine-tuning a GitHub-issue-fixing agent. [2]

Sources
[1] SWE-BENCH: CAN LANGUAGE MODELS RESOLVE REAL-WORLD GITHUB ISSUES?, ABSTRACT, p. 1
[2] SWE-BENCH: CAN LANGUAGE MODELS RESOLVE REAL-WORLD GITHUB ISSUES?, 1 INTRODUCTION, p. 1
